data.title: New York Times agrees to license content to Amazon for AI use
data.description:
(May 29): The New York Times Co has reached a deal to license its editorial content to Amazon.com Inc for use across its artificial intelligence platforms, marking the first such agreement the newspaper company has struck after years of battles between the media and a new, disruptive technology.

The multi-year collaboration “will bring Times editorial content to a variety of Amazon customer experiences,” the news outlet said in a statement Thursday. In addition to editorial content, the deal will include recipes from NYT Cooking and The Athletic, which focuses on sports.

“This broadens the companies’ existing relationship, and will bring additional value to Amazon customers and bring Times journalism to wider audiences,” the publisher said.

Summaries and short excerpts of Times content will be displayed within Amazon products and services, such as the Alexa voice platform, and used to train Amazon’s proprietary AI models, according to the statement.

Terms of the deal weren’t disclosed, and Amazon declined to comment.

The New York Times remains embroiled in a lawsuit with OpenAI and its partner Microsoft Corp over copyright infringement. The Times sued the tech companies in 2023, accusing them of using millions of articles published by the newspaper to train chatbots without compensation.

OpenAI has been accused of scraping text across the web to train its popular ChatGPT chatbot since it debuted in 2022. The AI start-up has been sued by prominent authors and other news publishers for breach of copyright. The narrative-style answers that AI tools like ChatGPT provide rival, and sometimes replicate, news articles, which has caused publishers concern about losing readers and advertising revenue. Dozens of publishers have signed lucrative licensing deals with OpenAI, Alphabet Inc and Perplexity AI in order to wrest back some of that lost business.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: Global air cargo demand up by 5.8% y-o-y in April — IATA
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): The International Air Transport Association (IATA) has reported a year-on-year (y-o-y) increase in global air cargo demand for April 2025.

Total demand, measured in cargo tonne-kilometres (CTK), rose by 5.8% in volume, building on March's solid performance, IATA said in a statement on Thursday.

International operations saw an even stronger increase of 6.5%, while in terms of capacity, measured in available cargo tonne-kilometres (ACTK), ticked up by 6.3% globally and 6.9% for international routes.

In April 2024, the total demand for international operations was 6.5% higher. In terms of capacity, measured in ACTK, increased by 6.3%, compared with April 2024 of 6.9% for international operations. 

IATA director general Willie Walsh said seasonal demand for fashion and consumer goods, front-loading ahead of the US tariff changes, and lower jet fuel prices have combined to boost air cargo. 

"With available capacity at record levels and yields improving, the outlook for air cargo is encouraging. While April brought good news, stresses in world trade are no secret. 

"Shifts in trade policy, particularly in the US, are already reshaping demand and export dynamics. Airlines will need to remain flexible as the situation develops over the coming months," he said. 

Several economic indicators supported the uptick in air cargo performance, including the increase in global industrial production, which rose 3.2% y-o-y in March.

It also noted that jet fuel prices declined by 21.2% from the previous year and 4.1% compared with March, marking the third consecutive month of falling fuel costs.

Besides, global manufacturing Purchasing Managers’ Index (PMI) rose to 50.5 in April, signalling continued expansion for the fourth month in a row.

"However, there are also signs of caution. The PMI for new export orders dropped 2.8 points to 47.2, remaining below the 50-point benchmark that indicates growth," it said. 

In terms of regional performance in April, Asia-Pacific airlines saw 10% y-o-y demand growth for air cargo, while capacity increased by 9.4% y-o-y. 

On trade lane growth, IATA said all international routes experienced growth in April, except for Middle East-Europe, Africa-Asia, and intra-European routes.

Overall, the data reflect a robust air cargo sector buoyed by economic and seasonal tailwinds, though the potential for policy-driven shifts remains a key risk in the months ahead.

The IATA represents some 350 airlines comprising over 80 per cent of global air traffic.

In terms of total cargo traffic measured by CTK, the Asia-Pacific region holds the largest market share at 34.2%. North America follows with 25.8%, while Europe accounts for 21.5% of the global share.

The Middle East contributes 13.6% to the total cargo traffic, whereas Latin America and Africa represent smaller portions, with 2.9% and 2%, respectively. These figures highlight the dominant role of Asia-Pacific, North America, and Europe in global air cargo movement.

Uploaded by Felyx Teoh

data.title: Musk's Trump administration exit lifts Tesla shares on hopes of renewed focus
data.description:
(May 29): Tesla shares rose more than 2% on Thursday after news that CEO Elon Musk was stepping away from the Trump administration, fuelling hopes that he will sharpen focus on the automaker as it gears up for its hotly anticipated robotaxi debut.

Musk's ties with US President Donald Trump and his tilt toward right-wing politics in Europe had fueled waves of protests against Tesla in recent months, pushing some buyers away and rattling investors worried about brand damage.

With Tesla on track for a second straight annual sales decline after its first-ever drop in 2024, pressure was mounting on the world's richest man — who also owns SpaceX and X — to refocus on the automaker that underpins much of his fortune.

Musk signalled recently he was cutting back on his role with the Department of Government Efficiency, telling shareholders in March he would reduce his time to a day or two per week.

He also announced last week that he will cut his political spending substantially. Musk spent nearly US$300 million (RM1.2 billion) to back Trump's presidential campaign and other Republicans last year.

Musk's departure from the administration "is music to the ears of Tesla shareholders," said Wedbush Securities analyst Dan Ives, a long-time Tesla bull who had last month called the Tesla chief's time in Washington a "code red situation" that could create lasting demand destruction.

"2025 started off as a dark chapter for Musk and Tesla," he added, "but importantly those days are in the rear-view mirror."

Tesla shares, trading at US$363 before the bell, have declined 11% this year.

News of Musk's departure was shortly followed by a post on X from Musk announcing that Tesla has been testing driverless Model Y cars in Austin, Texas, with no incidents and aims to deliver the first unit in June.

The roll-out is crucial for Tesla as Musk has shifted the company's focus away from building a new, cheaper electric vehicle platform to launching the robotaxi service and its Optimus humanoid robots. Much of Tesla's valuation hangs on that bet.

"Musk's departure from DOGE will improve market sentiment for Tesla, but I see no real change for Tesla," Morningstar analyst Seth Goldstein said.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: AmanahRaya REIT to buy industrial asset in Selangor for RM39m
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Amanahraya Real Estate Investment Trust (AmanahRaya REIT) (KL:ARREIT) is acquiring an industrial building, measuring 132,700 sq ft, in Telok Panglima Garang, Selangor for RM39 million.

Pacific Trustees Bhd, acting on behalf AmanahRaya REIT, entered into an agreement on Thursday to buy the property from Alpha Express Sdn Bhd, said AmanahRaya REIT in an exchange filing.

It noted that the RM39 million price tag reflects a slight discount from the property’s RM40 million market valuation as of May 2.

Upon completion, the property will be leased back to the seller, Alpha Express, under a 10-year triple net lease at RM195,000 per month, subject to rent reversion for each renewal term.

“The proposed acquisition of the property will be accretive to AmanahRaya REIT’s distributable income and adds to the portfolio of properties that will benefit the fund in the long term,” said the REIT, which is 45.6%-owned by Minister of Finance Inc trustee company Amanah Raya Bhd.

AmanahRaya REIT noted that the property is located near the South Klang Valley Expressway and the West Coast Expressway.

The REIT said it will fund the purchase via bank borrowings. As at end-March 2025, its cash stood at RM24.63 million, while borrowings totalled RM571.88 million.

The transaction is expected to close by the fourth quarter of 2025, it added.

Shares in AmanahRaya REIT closed up one sen or 2.67% to 38.5 sen on Thursday, valuing the trust at RM220.70 million.

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: US corporate profits fell by most since 2020 ahead of tariffs
data.description:
(May 29): US corporate profits fell in the first quarter by the most since 2020, indicating large companies were already feeling some pressure prior to the Trump administration’s sweeping tariffs on global trading partners.

The 2.9% decrease in profits followed a 5.4% advance in the fourth quarter, according to Bureau of Economic Analysis data out Thursday. Despite the drop, profits remained well above historical norms relative to gross domestic product, which fell 0.2%.

The extent to which American companies choose to raise consumer prices in response to tariffs on imported goods has become a key question for the US economic outlook in 2025. Wall Street economists generally expect inflation to pick up this year, hitting disposable household incomes and undercutting growth.

President Donald Trump recently singled out Walmart Inc in a social media post after the retail giant warned of coming price increases. The president highlighted the company’s profits, arguing it should “‘EAT THE TARIFFS,’ and not charge valued customers ANYTHING.”

Details of Thursday’s report showed a measure of after-tax profits for nonfinancial firms as a share of gross value added — a proxy for margins — edged down to 15.7% from 15.9%, remaining well above levels that prevailed from 1951 to 2019. The modest decline suggests large companies still have some room to absorb higher costs from tariffs without passing those on to consumers.

Uploaded by Chng Shear Lane

data.title: Wasco eyes recurring income stream from biomass plant ownership, says CEO
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Wasco Bhd (KL:WASCO), which specialises in oil and gas (O&G) pipes, is looking to cultivate recurring income from its biomass power plants.

Managing director and group chief executive officer Giancarlo Maccagno said the group intends to deepen its footprint in the bioenergy segment by evolving from a pure engineering, procurement, construction, and commissioning (EPCC) player to an asset owner.

“The plan is to move beyond just building plants for others. If we can lock in long-term feedstock supply and secure offtake agreements — for example selling energy to Tenaga Nasional (KL:TENAGA) — we could create a stable, recurring income stream,” Maccagno told reporters after its 25th annual general meeting.

“This is a natural extension of our core competencies and highly complementary to our existing renewable energy business,” he added.

Wasco currently undertakes full EPCC scope for biomass projects, including the supply of steam turbines, mainly catering to the palm oil sector. The group sees strong prospects in Malaysia and Indonesia, where empty fruit bunches (EFB) from palm oil mills are an underutilised source of renewable energy.

Maccagno, however, was quick to point out that the group would only proceed when both supply and demand sides have been secured.

"The risk lies in the feedstock provider walking away or the buyer defaulting. We will not rush into it until all the boxes are ticked,” he said, noting project costs could range between RM30 million and RM40 million per plant.

The group is not committing to a timeline for its maiden investment, preferring to wait for the right opportunity. “We are not under pressure. Our current business is doing well. But this biomass strategy represents a very exciting future because it allows us to reduce the project-based volatility in our revenue,” he added.

The push into biomass aligns with Malaysia’s evolving energy policy, which is increasingly supportive of renewable sources beyond solar, including biomass and small hydro.

As at end-FY2024, Wasco held a group order book of RM2.5 billion, with 85% derived from the O&G segment and the remaining 15% from renewables. Its tender book currently stands at RM13 billion.

Read also:
Wasco CEO warns of lower earnings in FY2025 due to project delays from market uncertainties

Edited ByKathy Fong

data.title: Dagang NeXchange swings to loss in 1Q on higher interest expense
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Dagang NeXchange Bhd (KL:DNEX) has posted a net loss of RM79.04 million for the first quarter, compared with a net profit of RM14.46 million a year earlier, mainly due to a higher interest expense.

The company recorded an income tax expense of RM115.65 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025), against an income tax credit of RM1.43 million previously. Deferred tax liabilities rose to RM868.16 million from RM766.10 million.

The increase in deferred tax liabilities was mainly due to the UK Energy Profits Levy (EPL), after a bill extending the levy from March 31, 2028 to March 31, 2030, was effectively passed on March 3, said DNeX in its bourse filing on Thursday.

In comparison, the same quarter last year saw a reversal of deferred tax liabilities from remeasuring EPL-related temporary differences.

The EPL was first introduced by the UK government in July 2022 as a temporary tax of 25% on profits from upstream oil and gas activities. The rate was later raised to 35%, starting from 2023 until March 31, 2028. On July 29, 2024, the government announced another increase, raising the EPL rate to 38%, effective from Nov 1, 2024.

DNeX's 90%-owned unit Ping Petroleum wholly owns UK-based upstream oil and gas firm Ping Petroleum UK plc.

DNeX did not declare any dividend for 1QFY2025.

The company's quarterly revenue fell 4.19% to RM296.83 million from RM309.82 million in 1QFY2024, mainly due to weaker contributions from the energy and information technology segments, which offset gains in the technology segment.

Revenue from the technology segment rose 30% to RM178.71 million from RM137.96 million a year ago, driven by an improved product mix and stronger market demand. This was further supported by growing demand for emerging technologies, which made up 37% of the product mix, compared to 19% a year earlier.

The average selling price per wafer rose significantly to US$589 (RM2,490), up from US$467 a year ago. Wafer shipments also increased to 63,400 units, compared to 56,600 units in 1QFY2024.

Revenue from the energy segment fell 26% to RM78.81 million from RM105.93 million in 1QFY2024, mainly due to lower average crude oil prices and slightly reduced lifting volumes.

Crude oil was sold at US$74.30 per barrel in 1QFY2025, down 15% from US$87.70 in the same quarter last year. Lifting volume also fell 7% year-on-year to 175,000 barrels, further weighing on revenue performance.

Revenue from the information technology segment dropped 40% to RM39.31 million, primarily due to the absence of contributions from the subsea telco business following its divestment.

Looking ahead to 2025, the company said its 60%-owned unit Silterra Malaysia Sdn Bhd is expanding its capabilities in silicon photonics, an enabling technology for high-speed, energy-efficient data transmission in hyperscale data centres. This innovation is crucial for next-generation AI and cloud computing applications, providing faster data processing with significantly lower power consumption.

"SilTerra is experiencing growing demand for technologies such as MEMS on CMOS, Life Sciences, and Smart Power — serving critical use cases in smart devices, biomedical instrumentation, electric vehicles, and renewable energy systems," the company added.

For its energy segment, the company said Ping continues to operate the producing Anasuria field while planning the development of greenfield assets — Fyne, Avalon, and Pilot — alongside a rejuvenation plan for its brownfield Hutton asset.

Despite a softer crude oil price environment, DNeX's disciplined cost structure and focus on production efficiency are expected to maintain profitability, the company said.

For its information technology segment, DNeX said it will continue to expand its business beyond government-driven projects to include more private sector digital transformation initiatives.

Shares of DNeX closed flat at 29.5 sen, giving the company a market capitalisation of RM1.02 billion. The counter has declined 26.25% so far this year.

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: DeepSeek says upgraded model reasons better, hallucinates less
data.description:
(May 29): The Chinese startup DeepSeek said Thursday that its upgraded artificial intelligence (AI) model can perform mathematics, programming, and general logic better than the previous version, while hallucinating less.

The upgrade to its R1 model — which stunned the AI world in January by rivalling the systems of much-larger US developers despite being built at what the Chinese startup said was a fraction of the cost — features a greater depth of reasoning, DeepSeek said in a post on the AI model platform Hugging Face. 

“Its overall performance is now approaching that of leading models,” such as OpenAI’s o3 and Google’s Gemini 2.5 Pro, the company said. 

The Hangzhou-based startup rocked the global tech industry in January when it unveiled the original R1, throwing into question the worldwide flood of spending on AI computational resources and touching off a flurry of AI releases among Chinese players from Alibaba Group Holding Ltd to Zhipu AI.

The debut of R1 turned DeepSeek founder Liang Wenfeng into a tech celebrity and a symbol of China’s ability to compete with the best of Silicon Valley. In February, President Xi Jinping invited Liang to a high-profile gathering with some of the country’s most prominent entrepreneurs. The young founder was seated among the likes of Alibaba co-founder Jack Ma and Tencent Holdings Ltd’s Pony Ma.

DeepSeek’s update to R1, known as DeepSeek-R1-0528, was announced on Wednesday — just hours before the latest financial report from Santa Clara, California-based Nvidia Corp, the leading maker of AI chips whose shares were pummelled in the immediate wake of R1’s release. Nvidia’s fortunes have recovered since, as AI data centre investment has continued at a strong pace, and the US company offered a solid forecast for the current quarter. 

Uploaded by Chng Shear Lane

data.title: Tropicana reports second consecutive profitable quarter in 1QFY2025
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Tropicana Corp Bhd (KL:TROP) remained profitable for two consecutive quarters. The property developer posted a net profit of RM1.32 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025), lifted by a tax credit of RM6.63 million.

It incurred a net loss of RM9.08 million in 1QFY2024, when the property developer incurred a tax expense of RM16.54 million, according to its filing with Bursa Malaysia on Thursday.

Quarterly revenue, however, fell 10.61% year-on-year to RM260.36 million from RM291.28 million, attributed to lower recurring income following the completion of several investment property disposals. 

Notably, the developer’s finance costs came in lower at RM31.49 million compared with RM43.9 million a year ago. 

No dividend was declared for 1QFY2025.

Tropicana’s current land bank stands at 1,336.1 acres, with a potential gross development value (GDV) of RM168.4 billion. Its unbilled sales stood at RM2.1 billion. 

The group said it expects to sustain market traction with eight ongoing and upcoming projects worth an estimated GDV of RM6.2 billion. These include Varia Shop Offices at Tropicana Aman in Kota Kemuning, Avisa Terrace Homes at Tropicana Alam in Puncak Alam, and Breeze Hill Shoppes & Serviced Apartments at Tropicana Avalon in Tropicana WindCity, Genting Highlands.

“We will continue to strive forward and strengthen our stakeholder engagements, focusing on value creation as well as establishing strategic marketing and sales campaigns across our online and offline platforms,” Tropicana said in a statement. 

“We believe that the demand for properties in Tropicana’s established, mature and developing townships will persist and that the property market should maintain its positive momentum in 2025,” the group added. 

Shares of Tropicana gained one sen to close at RM1.20 on Thursday, valuing it at RM2.91 billion. 


~end~

Edited ByKathy Fong

data.title: Germany seeks to levy 10% tax on online platforms like Google
data.description:
BERLIN (May 29): Germany is considering a 10% tax on large online platforms like Alphabet's Google and Meta's Facebook, its new culture minister told magazine Stern, in a move likely to heighten trade tensions with the Trump administration.

The proposal comes as Chancellor Friedrich Merz is expected to travel to Washington soon to meet with US President Donald Trump, although a trip has not yet been officially announced. Trump has in the past said he will not allow foreign governments to "appropriate America's tax base for their own benefit".

Culture Minister Wolfram Weimer said his ministry was drafting a legislative proposal while also seeking talks with platform operators that he accused of "cunning tax evasion" to explore alternative solutions like voluntary contributions.

"These corporations do billions in business in Germany with extremely high profit margins and benefit enormously from the country’s media and cultural output as well as its infrastructure — but they pay hardly any taxes, invest too little, and give far too little back to society," he told Stern in an interview published on Thursday.

Alphabet and Meta did not immediately respond to Reuters requests for comment.

Germany's ruling parties agreed in a coalition deal earlier this year to impose such a digital services levy.

If the government goes ahead with the tax on sales revenue generated by digital services providers within its borders, it would join a raft of other countries to have done so such as Britain, France, Italy, Spain, Turkey, India, Austria and Canada.

During Trump's first term, the US Trade Representative's office launched a Section 301 investigation into unfair trade practices against several of these countries, finding they discriminated against US companies, paving the way for retaliatory tariffs on certain imports.

In February, Trump ordered his trade chief to revive investigations aimed at imposing tariffs on imports from countries that levy digital service taxes on US technology companies.

That does not, however, appear to have deterred the new German government, which took office earlier this month.

Weimer accused the big digital platforms of building up "monopoly-like structures" that not only restrict competition but also concentrate media power too heavily", posing a risk for freedom of expression.

"If Google, under pressure from Donald Trump, unilaterally renames the Gulf of Mexico to the Gulf of America— and simply decrees this due to its enormous power to shape meaning in global communication — then we can see the kinds of problems that lie within the current structures," he said.

Uploaded by Chng Shear Lane

data.title: Hartanah Kenyalang IPO oversubscribed 5.33 times ahead of ACE Market listing
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Hartanah Kenyalang Bhd’s initial public offering (IPO) has been oversubscribed by 5.33 times, ahead of its listing on the ACE Market of Bursa Malaysia on June 9.

The construction company received a total of 5,119 applications for 196.2 million IPO shares worth RM31.4 million, from the Malaysian public for 31 million IPO shares made available to the public, which represented an oversubscription rate of 5.33 times.

“A total of 15.5 million IPO shares had been set aside for allotment under the Bumiputera Malaysian public category, which represented 50% of the IPO shares available for application by the Malaysian Public. The remaining 15.5 million IPO shares were allotted under the other Malaysian public category,” it said in a stock exchange filing.

For the Bumiputera Malaysian public category, a total of 2,394 applications for 61.08 million IPO shares were received, which represented an oversubscription rate of 2.94 times.

For the other Malaysian public category, a total of 2,725 applications for 135.1 million IPO shares were received, which represented an oversubscription rate of 7.72 times

The company said the aggregate of 42.6 million IPO shares (including 77.5 million IPO shares made available for application by way of private placement to identified Bumiputera investors) was approved by the Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry (Miti).

The remaining 38.86 million IPO shares, which were initially reserved for application by identified Bumiputera investors approved by Miti, have been fully placed out “after clawback and reallocation pursuant to the prospectus”.

Under the private placement to selected investors and identified Bumiputera investors approved by the Miti, a total of 113.94 million IPO shares had been allocated to a total of 515 investors.

The company has launched its prospectus en route to its IPO on Bursa Malaysia’s ACE Market to raise RM19.3 million based on its IPO price of 16 sen per share.

Uploaded by Felyx Teoh

data.title: Genting's 1Q net profit down sharply amid lower revenues from casinos and higher taxation
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Genting Bhd’s (KL:GENTING) net profit shrank to only RM4.57 million for the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025), from RM588.87 million a year ago. 

The sharp fall in net profit was partly due to lower quarterly revenue, which fell 12.4% to RM6.51 billion from RM7.43 billion in 1QFY2024. The group’s hospitality segment generated a lower contribution of RM5.34 billion in the quarter under review compared with RM6.48 billion a year ago, according to its bourse filing. 

Meanwhile, the group’s other income halved to RM257 million versus RM532 million a year ago, and losses from its share of results in joint ventures and associates also doubled to RM28 million. 

Furthermore, it incurred taxation of RM348.61 million, which was roughly 9% lower compared with RM381.8 million a year ago although its pre-tax profit contracted substantially to RM626.2 million versus RM1.38 billion in 1QFY2024. 

Notably, the group booked a higher impairment loss of RM72 million compared with RM6.21 million a year earlier.

Earnings per share contracted to 0.12 sen from 15.29 sen a year ago.

No dividend was declared for the quarter.

In a press statement, Genting noted that while demand for international tourism is expected to remain resilient, the recovery is likely to be uneven across regions amid ongoing global uncertainties. As a result, the regional gaming market could face mounting challenges.

The group remains cautiously optimistic about the near-term outlook for the leisure and hospitality industry and maintains a positive view on its long-term prospects.

"Amid heightened geopolitical trade tensions and macroeconomic headwinds, Genting Singapore Ltd Group continues to adopt a prudent and adaptive approach, leveraging on its strong regional presence and financial strength to navigate through a challenging global environment," said Genting.

Genting shares fell five sen to close at RM3.13 on Thursday, giving the group a market capitalisation of RM12.1 billion. The stock has fallen 32.5% over the past 12 months from RM4.64.

Edited ByKathy Fong

data.title: QL Resources’ 4Q net profit down 5%; flags earnings risk from egg subsidy removal
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Egg producer QL Resources Bhd (KL:QL) said its net profit for the fourth quarter ended March 31, 2025 (4QFY2025) declined 4.92% to RM93.92 million from RM98.78 million a year earlier, due to lower operating profit.

Quarterly revenue rose 6.73% year-on-year (y-o-y) to RM1.76 billion from RM1.65 billion, supported by stronger contributions across all segments, particularly the palm oil and clean energy segment, the group said in its bourse filing on Thursday.

QL Resources said its palm oil and clean energy segment benefitted from the consolidation of results from the newly acquired Plus Xnergy Holdings and increased solar project deliveries under BM Greentech. Additionally, palm oil activities recorded better performance, driven by significantly higher crude palm oil prices.

The group has proposed a final dividend of 2.50 sen per share, amounting to RM91.3 million, bringing total dividend payout for FY2025 to five sen per share — lower than the 6.5 sen per share declared in the previous year.

For the full year, QL Resources posted a 4.05% increase in net profit to RM455.59 million from RM437.85 million in FY2024. Revenue climbed 6.34% to RM7.07 billion from RM6.65 billion, as all business segments recorded an improved performance.

Looking ahead, QL Resources warned of downside risks stemming from external uncertainties, notably the US' reciprocal tariffs, noting that it may trigger tighter trade policies, disrupt investments and spending, and cause foreign exchange volatility.

The group also noted that its integrated livestock farming segment is expected to face lower profitability due to the government’s move to gradually remove subsidies on chicken eggs. Effective May 1, the subsidy rate was halved to five sen per egg from 10 sen previously. The full removal of subsidies, along with the elimination of price ceilings, is slated for August 1.

The livestock farming segment was its largest contributor, accounting for 52% of the group’s total revenue for FY2025. This was followed by marine product manufacturing at 20%, convenience store chain at 17%, and palm oil and clean energy at 11%.

For the marine product manufacturing segment, its outlook is “neutral to positive” supported by low input costs and increased export demand for surimi-based products, which remain competitively positioned under the current tariff regime, said QL Resources.

QL Resources added that its convenience store chain segment will continue expanding, with new outlets planned in underserved areas. The group also aims to introduce more local delicacies to offer consumers better value.

“With our core businesses dealing with basic foods and green energy solution, the management nevertheless remains cautiously optimistic that the group's business performance will remain resilient for the new financial year notwithstanding continued uncertainty in the regional economies and Malaysia egg subsidy rationalisation,” QL Resources added.

Shares of QL Resources closed down five sen or 1.09% to RM4.54 on Thursday, giving the group a market capitalisation of RM16.75 billion.

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: SunCon bags two data centre contracts worth RM1.16 bil from US-based tech firm
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Sunway Construction Group Bhd (KL:SUNCON) has secured two work orders valued at RM1.16 billion to undertake general contractor services for two data centre projects awarded by a US-based technology company. 

The projects are scheduled to begin immediately, with final completion targeted for February 2027, SunCon said in its filing. 

SunCon has secured RM3.5 billion in new orders so far this year, representing over half of its 2025 order book replenishment target range of RM4.5 billion to RM6 billion. Consequently, its total outstanding order book has increased to RM7.9 billion.

“The projects win further expand the group’s order book for the year and fortify earnings visibility over the next two years.

We are confident that this positive momentum will continue in the current financial year, underpinned by accelerated progress on data centre projects and a robust outstanding order book, including newly secured projects,” said SunCon managing director Liew Kok Wing in a separate statement.  

The contracts were awarded to its wholly owned unit Sunway Construction Sdn Bhd (SCSB). The identity of the awarding company was not disclosed.

SunCon shares closed 12 sen or 2.02% lower at RM5.83, valuing the group at RM7.55 billion. Year to date, the stock has gained nearly 26%.
 

Edited ByAdam Aziz

data.title: Ajinomoto sees cost swings persisting after first quarterly loss since 2022
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Ajinomoto (M) Bhd (KL:AJI) slipped into the red in the fourth quarter ended March 31, 2025 (4QFY2025) with a net loss of RM6.9 million compared with a net profit of RM364.21 million a year earlier, mainly due to higher selling and administrative expenses.

Revenue for the quarter declined 1.95% year-on-year (y-o-y) to RM149.79 million from RM152.76 million previously, dragged by weaker industrial segment performance amid lower sales volume and pricing.

The company attributed the quarterly operating loss of RM543,000 — a reversal from a RM12.08 million profit in 4QFY2024 — to a spike in advertising and promotional spending.

The company said it expects raw material and logistic cost volatility to persist due to geopolitical tensions, but plans to bolster its competitive edge to maintain market share and profitability.

Against the immediate preceding quarter (3QFY2025), the group posted a net loss versus a RM30.89 million net profit previously, as revenue fell 16.70% from RM179.79 million, also due to lower sales in both consumer and industrial segments.

For the full year FY2025, net profit slumped 87.63% to RM49.66 million from RM401.42 million in FY2024, despite revenue rising 7.55% to RM684.50 million.

By geography, Malaysia remained the largest market with RM97.4 million revenue in 4QFY2025, followed by RM23.40 million from the Middle East and RM27.95 million from other Asian countries.

Ajinomoto’s shares fell 28 sen or 1.9% to RM14.24 in the final hour of trading on Thursday, giving it a market capitalisation of RM865.77 million. The counter is down 6% this year.

data.title: Perak pushes for growth with sea, halal, AI hubs and food security deals at Asean Summit 2025
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Perak plans to build a big sea terminal, creating a halal and artificial intelligence (AI) industry hub, and improving food security in its bid to become a key economic centre.

At the Asean Summit 2025, the state through Perak State Development Corporation (PKNPk) signed two deals with the United Arab Emirates (UAE) government and CHEC Construction Sdn Bhd. The focus is on three main areas: developing the Bagan Datuk International Sea-Hub Multipurpose Terminal (BDI-SMT), setting up a halal and AI industrial hub, and partnering on food security.

In a statement, PKNPk said the memorandum of understanding with the UAE government sets a plan to work together on sustainable food security and smart investments linked to the BDI-SMT project. This includes exploring new farming technologies, improving regional trade and strengthening supply chains.

The agreement with CHEC Construction, a global infrastructure company, plans to develop the BDI-SMT as a major trade hub connecting China, Asean and Gulf Cooperation Council countries. It also includes creating a halal and AI industrial hub in Perak to boost ethical manufacturing, digital industries and new economic growth.

Perak Corporation Bhd (KL:PRKCORP) will lead the BDI-SMT development, supporting Perak’s goal to grow its Lumut Maritime Industrial City, focusing on maritime, logistics and industry. This project aligns with national goals and will boost Malaysia’s economy and infrastructure.

PKNPk chief executive officer Datuk Redza Rafiq highlighted that these commercially driven partnerships will create jobs, stimulate industry and boost infrastructure while attracting foreign direct investment, particularly from China and the UAE. The BDI-SMT is set to become a key maritime trade hub in Asia, with added focus on halal manufacturing and digital industry growth.

PKNPk also said the strategic alliances signal Perak’s readiness not only to embrace future economic trends but also to actively shape them, driving a new era of growth that is globally connected and sustainably developed.

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title: Wasco CEO warns of lower earnings in FY2025 due to project delays from market uncertainties
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Energy infrastructure company Wasco Bhd (KL:WASCO) expects slower growth for the year ending December 31, 2025, due to global economic uncertainties and project delays.

Group CEO Giancarlo Maccagno said demand is still strong, but geopolitical risks and unclear policies, especially US trade tariffs, are causing clients to delay decisions.

“We’ve seen a bit of a slowdown in the last few months. Clients are hesitating, waiting for more clarity on trade policies and tariffs. It’s not that the demand isn’t there — it is — but decisions are being delayed,” he told reporters after the group’s 25th annual general meeting on Thursday.

Maccagno said it would be tough to match Wasco’s record results from FY2024, when net profit rose 41% to RM153 million and revenue increased 33% to RM3.2 billion.

“FY2025 will still be a good year — it will be profitable — but to exceed FY2024 will be tough, especially with current utilisation levels not yet maximised,” he said. “Some projects may only pick up speed towards the end of this year, or even be pushed into 2026.”

The group has RM2.5 billion in confirmed orders — 85% from energy contracts and 15% from renewables — giving good revenue visibility for 2025. Its tender book is also large, at RM13 billion.

Maccagno believes natural gas and hydrocarbons will stay important for global energy supply for the next 20 to 30 years.

“There’s no way to meet the world’s growing energy demand — especially with the rise of data centres and electric mobility — without hydrocarbons. Gas, in particular, will be the transition fuel,” he said.

“Some projects may be delayed, yes. But the business is there. The energy transition is happening, and we’re right in the middle of it.”
 

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title: Leong Hup’s 1Q profit rises 80% as higher selling prices boost margins
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Integrated poultry player Leong Hup International Bhd (KL:LHI) reported a 79.92% increase in net profit for the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025), driven primarily by higher average selling prices and reduced operating costs, with contributions mainly from Malaysia, Vietnam and the Philippines.

For the quarter, the group recorded a net profit of RM101.80 million, compared to RM56.58 million in the same period a year earlier. Earnings per share rose to 2.81 sen from 1.55 sen previously. 

The company declared a first interim single-tier dividend of one sen per share, payable on July 1. 

However, quarterly revenue fell by 8.30% year-on-year to RM2.21 billion from RM2.41 billion. The decline was attributed to weaker performance in the Malaysian and Singaporean markets, which offset gains in the Philippines. 

According to a filing with Bursa Malaysia on Thursday, revenue in the Philippines increased due to stronger average selling prices and higher sales volumes of dressed chicken. Nonetheless, this growth was largely offset by revenue reductions in Malaysia and Singapore. 

In Malaysia, although average selling prices of day-old chicks and broiler chickens improved, revenue was impacted by a decline in egg prices. Meanwhile, in Singapore, the group reported a fall in revenue due to lower selling prices and volumes of fresh chicken, as well as a decrease in duck sales volume. 

Looking ahead, Leong Hup anticipates margins to be supported by easing feed input costs, particularly corn and soybean meal. 

Leong Hup shares closed unchanged at 61 sen, valuing the group at RM2.20 billion. Year to date, the stock has gained 0.83%. 

Edited ByAdam Aziz

data.title: Shein to increase product safety testing after EU probe
data.description:
LONDON (May 29): Fast-fashion retailer Shein on Thursday said it would increase its testing of products this year, after the European Union warned of fines if it does not address the bloc's concerns about unsafe and dangerous products sold on its site.

Shein said it targets 2.5 million product safety and quality tests in 2025, up from two million last year, and said it would spend US$15 million (RM64 million) on compliance initiatives this year.

Shein, which sells its own-branded clothes in 150 countries, also operates a marketplace for sellers of toys, gadgets, and homeware sent directly from factories mostly in China to shoppers around the world.

Since it launched its marketplace, Shein has stopped working with more than 540 sellers over compliance breaches, the company said.

The EU's Consumer Protection Co-operation network of national consumer authorities and the European Commission on Monday notified Shein of practices that infringe EU consumer law, giving the company a month to reply.

Uploaded by Chng Shear Lane

data.title: PMB Technology’s 1Q net profit more than doubles despite drop in revenue
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Silicon metal and aluminium fabrication company PMB Technology Bhd (KL:PMBTECH) recorded a 123% year-on-year (y-o-y) increase in net profit for the first quarter, on better contribution from its silicon plant amid improved production efficiency.

The company posted a net profit of RM2.63 million for the quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025), compared with RM1.18 million previously. Earnings per share doubled to 14 sen from seven sen, according to a filing with Bursa Malaysia on Thursday. 

No dividend was declared for the quarter.

Despite the improved profitability, quarterly revenue declined 40.80% y-o-y to RM156.45 million from RM264.27 million, attributed to lower overall contributions from both the manufacturing and trading segment as well as the construction and fabrication segment.

Revenue from the manufacturing and trading segment fell 43% to RM115.0 million from RM201.4 million in 1QFY2024, primarily due to reduced sales volume of metallic silicon.

The construction and fabrication segment also saw a decrease in revenue, down 34% to RM41.4 million from RM62.9 million, which the group attributed to slower progress in certain ongoing projects.

PMB Technology, in its filing, expressed optimism about the prospects for the silicon metal industry, citing anticipated growth in global energy demand supported by an expansion in emerging economies and a broader transition towards electrification and digitalisation.

“With supply being gradually brought under control, it is a matter of time before the overhang in the solar industry gets resolved as demand continues to grow at a healthy pace,” the company added.

PMB Technology’s shares closed one sen or 0.8% lower at RM1.28on Thursfay, valuing the company  at RM2.39 billion. Year to date, the stock has retreated 26.01%.

Edited ByS Kanagaraju
